metadata.notes: Wingspan standard overhaul: removed legacy sections format, added rules_citations with key rulings, validated content against official rulebook.

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Global Components
- **1** game board with hex territories, Triumph Track, Popularity Track, and Power Track
- **1** quick-reference guide
- **5** player mats (Industrial, Engineering, Patriotic, Mechanical, Agricultural)
- **42** combat cards (values 2-5)
- **23** objective cards
- **28** encounter cards
- **12** Factory cards
- **80** resource tokens (20 each: food, metal, wood, oil)
- **2** power dials
- **80** cardboard coins
- **5** Riverwalk cards
- **12** multiplier tokens
- **12** encounter tokens
- **5** quick-start cards
- **6** structure bonus tiles

#### Per-Faction Components (x5 factions)
- **1** faction mat with unique ability and mech ability slots
- **1** character miniature
- **4** mech miniatures
- **8** workers
- **6** star tokens
- **4** structure tokens
- **4** recruit tokens
- **6** technology cubes
- **1** action, popularity, and power token each

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Core Setup
1. Place board centrally with Triumph, Popularity, and Power tracks visible
2. Place 1 encounter token on each encounter-marked territory (11 on board)
3. Create resource/coin supply near the board
4. Shuffle combat, encounter, and objective decks separately
5. Randomly choose 1 structure bonus tile, place at bottom of Popularity Track
6. Place Factory cards face down: players + 1 cards (e.g., 4 players = 5 Factory cards)

#### Faction/Mat Assignment
- Randomly deal 1 faction mat and 1 player mat per player
- Return unused mats and extra Factory cards to box

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Faction Mat Setup
- Place power token on starting value shown on faction mat
- Draw starting combat cards shown on faction mat
- Gain starting coins shown on faction mat

#### Player Mat Setup
- Place popularity token on starting value shown on player mat
- Draw starting objective cards shown on player mat
- Place 6 tech cubes on top-row green spaces with black-square markers
- Place 4 structures on structure boxes, 4 recruits on recruit spaces
- Place 6 reserve workers on Produce action rectangles
- Place action token beside player mat

#### Board Placement
- Place character on home base
- Place 2 workers on the 2 territories adjacent to home base
- Keep 4 mechs on faction mat mech spaces

#### Starting the Game
- Lowest-numbered player mat goes first, play clockwise
- Public info: track positions, board units, count of opponent combat cards
- Hidden info: combat card values, objective card content

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose action section** — Select 1 column on your player mat
2. **Repeat restriction** — You may NOT choose the same section as your previous turn
3. **Resolve top-row action** — Perform the top action (Move, Produce, Trade, or Bolster)
4. **Resolve bottom-row action** — Perform the paired bottom action if you can/want to pay (Upgrade, Deploy, Build, or Enlist)
5. **Star check** — Place a star immediately if you completed an achievement
6. **End turn** — Pass play clockwise

#### End Trigger
Game ends immediately when any player places their 6th star.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Top-Row Actions
- **Move** — Move up to 2 units (characters/mechs/workers) one territory each. Units can carry resources.
- **Produce** — Choose up to 2 territories with your workers. Each worker produces 1 resource matching the terrain: Farm=food, Forest=wood, Mountain=metal, Tundra=oil, Village=worker.
- **Trade** — Pay 1 coin to gain 2 resources of your choice, OR pay 1 coin to gain 1 popularity.
- **Bolster** — Gain power on the Power Track and/or draw combat cards.

#### Bottom-Row Actions
- **Upgrade (oil cost)** — Move 1 tech cube from a top-row action to a bottom-row action, improving the top-row benefit and reducing the bottom-row cost.
- **Deploy (metal cost)** — Place 1 mech from your faction mat to a territory with your workers. Deploying unlocks a faction-specific mech ability.
- **Build (wood cost)** — Place 1 structure from your player mat to a territory with your workers.
- **Enlist (food cost)** — Remove 1 recruit token, gain a one-time bonus, and gain an ongoing bonus that triggers whenever you OR an adjacent player takes that bottom-row action.

#### Key Rules
- You choose ONE column per turn — top and bottom must come from the same column
- Both actions are optional — you may skip either or both
- Resources are spent from territories you control (can combine across territories)
- Workers are NOT resources and cannot be spent as costs

subtopics.title: Combat
subtopics.content:
#### When Combat Occurs
Combat happens when your character or mech moves into a territory occupied by an opponent's combat units (characters/mechs). Workers alone don't trigger combat.

#### Combat Resolution
1. Both players simultaneously and secretly select power to spend (0 to their current power) on the power dial
2. Each player may add up to 1 combat card per their character/mech in the combat
3. Reveal simultaneously — highest total wins. Attacker wins ties.
4. Both players lose the power they bid (even the winner)

#### After Combat
- **Winner**: Stays on territory, places a combat star (max 2 per player; Saxony unlimited)
- **Loser**: All units retreat to home base. Resources on the territory remain and are claimed by the winner.
- **Popularity penalty**: Loser gains 1 combat card per retreating worker. Winner loses 1 popularity per retreating opponent worker.

#### Combat Notes
- Combat card count is public information — use it to estimate opponent strength
- Workers don't fight but their retreat costs the winner popularity
- You cannot attack someone on their home base

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Encounters
- Character reaches an encounter token → draw encounter card → choose 1 of 3 options
- Only characters resolve encounters (not mechs or workers)
- Encounter rewards are often worth a detour

#### The Factory
- Central hex on the board
- First time your character reaches it, choose 1 face-up Factory card
- Factory cards add a 5th action section to your player mat
- The Factory counts as 3 territories for end-game scoring
- Each player gets 1 Factory card per game

#### Structures
- 4 types with permanent effects:
  - **Mine**: Your character/mechs can use tunnels (move between any tunnel-marked territories)
  - **Mill**: Produce action on this territory costs no additional workers
  - **Armory**: Gain +1 power when you Bolster
  - **Monument**: Gain +1 popularity when you Bolster
- Structures stay on the board permanently
- You control a territory with your structure even without units (unless opponent units are present)

#### Stars (Achievements)
- Complete all 6 upgrades ★
- Deploy all 4 mechs ★
- Build all 4 structures ★
- Enlist all 4 recruits ★
- Place all 8 workers ★
- Complete 1 objective ★
- Win combat (max 2 stars; Saxony unlimited) ★★
- Reach 18 popularity ★
- Reach 16 power ★

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
Game ends immediately when any player places their 6th star.

#### Scoring Formula
Your final score = coins on hand + star coins + territory coins + resource coins + structure bonus

#### Popularity Multiplier (coins per item)
| Category | Tier 1 (0-6 pop) | Tier 2 (7-12 pop) | Tier 3 (13-18 pop) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Per star | 3 | 4 | 5 |
| Per territory | 2 | 3 | 4 |
| Per 2 resources | 1 | 2 | 3 |

#### Territory Counting
- Count territories with your units OR your structures (without opponent units)
- The Factory counts as 3 territories!
- Home base does NOT count as a territory

#### Structure Bonus
Score the randomly selected structure bonus tile based on your structure placement.

#### Tiebreakers
1. Most coins → 2. Most territories → 3. Most resources → 4. Most stars

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Priorities
- **Build a repeatable 2-3 turn loop.** Plan your first 3 turns so the no-repeat restriction never forces a dead turn.
- **Upgrade early.** One early Upgrade improves top-row output AND lowers a bottom-row cost simultaneously.
- **Expand workers onto productive terrain.** More workers = more resources = faster bottom-row completion = more stars.
- **Protect your resources.** Keep piles on territories you can hold, not exposed frontlines.
- **Track popularity from turn 1.** Popularity multiplies ALL end-game scoring categories.
- **Use encounters for tempo.** Route your character toward encounter tokens for free bonuses.

#### Common Mistakes
- Picking a strong action now without planning the mandatory different action next turn
- Chasing extra combats after hitting the 2 combat star cap
- Leaving large resource stacks on undefended frontier territories
- Forgetting private objectives until it's too late to pivot
- Neglecting popularity — strong board + Tier 1 popularity = lost game
- Triggering the 6th star while behind in territory/resources/popularity conversion

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Scythe — A Guided Walkthrough

**Setup Overview**
Each player gets a faction mat (your unique abilities and starting position) and a player mat (your action economy). The combination creates your unique strategic puzzle.

Place your character on your home base. Place 2 workers on the two adjacent territories. Start with resources shown on your faction mat (power, combat cards, coins) and player mat (popularity, objectives).

**Understanding Your Player Mat**
Your player mat has 4 columns. Each column pairs a top-row action with a bottom-row action. On your turn, pick ONE column — you can do the top, the bottom, or both. You CANNOT pick the same column you used last turn.

Look at the tech cubes on top-row actions — when you Upgrade, you move these cubes to bottom-row actions to reduce their costs.

**Turns 1-3: The Opening**

*Turn 1*: Find a column with **Produce** on top. Produce resources with your starting workers. If the bottom action is affordable, take it too!

*Turn 2*: Switch to a DIFFERENT column. Good options: **Trade** for resources you need, or **Bolster** for power. Take the bottom-row action if you can — **Upgrade** is excellent early.

*Turn 3*: Another different column. **Move** your character toward an encounter token. Encounters give free resources and bonuses!

**The Rhythm (Turns 4-10)**
You'll settle into a 2-turn loop: Produce on one turn, spend resources on the next. Every bottom-row action you complete progresses toward a star. Deploy mechs for mobility and combat. Build structures for permanent bonuses.

**Mid-Game: Combat and Territory**
With mechs deployed, you can contest territories. Before fighting, check your power vs opponent's power and count their combat cards. Only fight for valuable territory or when you need a combat star.

**End Game: The Star Race**
Watch opponent star counts! When someone hits 6 stars, the game ends IMMEDIATELY. Make sure you have good popularity (Tier 2+), territory control, and resources.

**First Game Target**: Focus on your 2-turn loop. Get 3-4 stars. Aim for Tier 2 popularity (7+). A score of 40-60 coins is solid.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Faction Strategy Guide

**Rusviet Union (Red)** — Top Tier
- Unique: Can repeat same player mat section two turns in a row
- This breaks the core restriction and makes Rusviet the most efficient faction
- Opening: Find your strongest column and spam it. Rusviet engines start faster than everyone else.
- Key mechs: Township (teleport to villages/Factory), People's Army (workers add +1 power in combat)

**Crimean Khanate (Yellow)** — High Tier
- Unique: Spend combat cards as any resource type
- Never resource-starved if you Bolster for combat cards
- Opening: Bolster heavily, then use combat cards to rush bottom-row actions
- Key mech: Scout (steal 1 opponent combat card before combat)

**Nordic Kingdoms (Blue)** — Mid Tier
- Unique: Workers swim across rivers without Riverwalk
- Fastest early map expansion — spread workers before anyone else can cross rivers
- Opening: Push workers across rivers onto diverse terrain types
- Key mech: Seaworthy (move across lakes), Artillery (-2 to opponent's combat power)

**Polania Republic (White)** — Mid-High Tier
- Unique: Choose 2 encounter options instead of 1
- Encounters become incredibly powerful — rush your character toward them
- Key mech: Camaraderie (no popularity loss when you displace opponent workers)

**Saxony Empire (Black)** — Variable Tier
- Unique: No limit on combat or objective stars
- Can rush 6 stars faster than anyone through aggressive combat
- Opening: Expand and fight. Every combat win = a star with no cap.
- Danger: If opponents avoid you, your aggression yields fewer stars

#### Player Mat Combos
- **Rusviet + Industrial**: Widely considered the strongest combo. Fast engine + repeat ability.
- **Crimea + Patriotic**: Resource flexibility + enlist bonuses = dominant mid-game.
- **Saxony + Mechanical**: Cheap mech deployment → early combat → unlimited combat stars.

#### Combat Strategy
- **Information advantage**: Combat card COUNT is public. Power is public. Calculate before fighting.
- **Spend minimum to win**: Overspending power is wasteful. Bid just enough.
- **Bluff zero**: Sometimes bidding 0 power is correct — opponent overspends, you lose nothing, and retreating workers give you a combat card.
- **Popularity cost**: Each displaced opponent worker = -1 popularity. Attacking territory with 3 workers costs 3 popularity (nearly a full tier!).

#### Star Rush vs Economic Play
- **Star Rush**: Place 6 stars ASAP to end the game while opponents build. Best for Saxony and Rusviet.
- **Economic Play**: Build Tier 3 popularity, hold many territories, stockpile resources. Let scoring multipliers win. Best for Nordic and Polania.
- **Balanced (recommended)**: 4-5 stars at good pace, Tier 2+ popularity, 5+ territories.

#### Key Decision Points
1. **Turns 1-3: Loop design** — Your 2-3 turn repeating pattern shapes the entire game.
2. **Turns 5-8: Factory timing** — Detour for a Factory card? Or stay local and produce? Factory = 3 territories + extra action.
3. **Mid-game: First combat** — The most impactful fight. Time it when you have power advantage.
4. **Late game: End timing** — Calculate your coin total before placing star #6. Would 1-2 more turns for better popularity/territory score higher?

#### Player Count Adjustments
- **2 Players**: Huge map, avoidable combat. Focus on engine efficiency and objectives.
- **3 Players**: Balanced. Each faction has room but territories start to matter.
- **4-5 Players**: Tight map, frequent combat. Popularity management is critical. Territory control drives scoring.

key_rulings.text: If the attacking player has the same total power as the defending player (including combat card values), the attacker wins.
You may not place your action token on the same section of your player mat as the previous turn. You must move your action token to a different section each turn.
Workers cannot move into a territory controlled by an opponent's workers, character, or mechs. Only characters and mechs can initiate combat by moving into an opponent-controlled territory.
At the end of the game, the Factory is worth a total of 3 territories (instead of 1) to the player who controls it.

key_rulings.topic: Home base is not a territory
key_rulings.text: Your home base is not a territory. It does not count for end-game scoring. You cannot move units into an opponent's home base.
If you win combat and your opponent has workers on the territory, each of those workers retreats to the opponent's home base, and you lose 1 popularity for each worker displaced.
Each player may place a maximum of 2 stars from winning combat. The Saxony Empire faction is the exception — they have no limit on the number of combat or objective stars they may place.
Resources are not owned by players. They are on specific territories. You control resources on territories where you have at least one unit (worker, character, or mech) or an uncontested structure.
If you produce on a territory with 1-2 workers, there is no additional cost. Producing on territories with 3-4 total workers costs 1 power. 5-6 workers costs 2 power. 7-8 workers costs 3 power.

A structure on a territory means you control that territory even without any units present — unless an opponent moves units onto that territory. Structures remain permanently on the board even if an opponent takes the territory.
